title: Rúnar Groth16 Witness-Assisted Verifying Key
description: Input schema for the `runarc groth16-wa` compiler backend. Describes a BN254 Groth16 verifying key in the Rúnar / SP1 convention: β, γ, δ stored PRE-NEGATED on the G2 side, and every Fp2 pair stored as (x0=real, x1=imag). All big-integer values are decimal strings. The alphaG1 field stores POSITIVE α; the compiler computes MillerLoop(α, -β) internally and bakes it into the script.

version.description: Source-identifying version tag, e.g. 'sp1-v6.0.0'. Preserved in the output artifact for downstream tooling; not parsed by the compiler.

curve.description: Elliptic curve identifier. Only 'bn254' is supported.

numPubInputs.description: Number of public inputs. Must equal len(ic) - 1.

convention.description: Free-form documentation field describing the G2 sign/order convention. Ignored by the compiler.

source.description: Optional free-form pointer to the upstream VK source (e.g. SP1 Solidity verifier file). Ignored by the compiler.

alphaG1.description: The α point of the verifying key, on G1. Stored POSITIVE: the compiler computes MillerLoop(α, -β) internally.

betaNegG2.description: The G2 point -β, pre-negated. Used by the compiler to compute MillerLoop(α, -β).

gammaNegG2.description: The G2 point -γ, pre-negated. Baked into the script as the second pairing's G2.

deltaNegG2.description: The G2 point -δ, pre-negated. Baked into the script as the third pairing's G2.

ic.description: Public-input commitment basis. Length MUST equal numPubInputs + 1: ic[0] is the constant term, ic[j+1] is multiplied by public input j. The compiler does NOT bake IC into the script; IC is used only by the off-chain witness generator to compute prepared_inputs via multi-scalar multiplication. Any hardcoding of IC would defeat the witness-assisted design.

DecimalBigInt.description: Non-negative big integer encoded as a base-10 string.

_comment.description: Optional human-readable comment, ignored by the compiler.

x0.description: Real part of x coordinate (x = x0 + x1*u in Fp2).

y0.description: Real part of y coordinate (y = y0 + y1*u in Fp2).
